```{r Import libraries}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| include: false
source("../common/load-neoipcr.R")
load_neoipcr(dev_pkg_path = "../../../neoipcr")
library(tidyverse, warn.conflicts = FALSE, quietly = TRUE)
library(smplot2, warn.conflicts = FALSE, quietly = TRUE)
library(scales, warn.conflicts = FALSE, quietly = TRUE)
library(patchwork, warn.conflicts = FALSE, quietly = TRUE)
library(gt, warn.conflicts = FALSE, quietly = TRUE)
```

```{r Set up functions}
#| include: false

source("../common/helpers.R")
source("../common/logging.R")
configure_logging(report = "reference-report")

labels_bw <- function(x) {
  paste(
    format_integer(x),
    sR$gramm_abbreviation,
    sep = sR$unit_separator)
}
breaks_bw <- function(x) {
  pretty(
    x,
    as.integer(
      (as.integer(x[2]) + 1 - as.integer(x[1])) / 100)
    )
}
labels_wk <- function(x) {
  paste(
    format(
      as.integer(x / 7),
      big.mark = sR$digit_group_separator
      ),
    sR$week_abbreviation,
    sep = sR$unit_separator)
}
breaks_wk <- function(x) {
  pretty(
    x / 7,
    as.integer((as.integer(x[2] / 7) + 1 - as.integer(x[1] / 7)) / 2)
    ) * 7
}

#' Detect if reference dataset contains countries from different World Bank classes
#' @param ref_countries Tibble with reference countries (name, wb_class)
#' @param include_wb_class Whether WB class is included ("no", "pseudo", "full")
#' @return List with has_mixed (logical) and class_names (character) or NULL
detect_mixed_wb_classes <- function(ref_countries, include_wb_class) {
  # Only detect when include_wb_class = "full"
  if (include_wb_class != "full" ||
      is.null(ref_countries)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  # For character vector, no WB class info available
  if (is.character(ref_countries) ||
      !("wb_class" %in% names(ref_countries))) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  # Get unique WB classes
  ref_classes <- ref_countries |>
    dplyr::pull("wb_class") |>
    as.character() |>
    unique() |>
    sort()

  # Check if more than one class
  has_mixed <- length(ref_classes) > 1

  if (!has_mixed) {
    return(list(has_mixed = FALSE))
  }

  # Get localized display names for all classes
  class_names <- get_localised_world_bank_class_names(ref_classes) |>
    paste(collapse = ", ")

  return(list(
    has_mixed = TRUE,
    class_names = class_names
  ))
}

```

```{r Initialise locale dependent information}
#| include: false

locale <- Sys.getenv("LC_ALL")
localeObj <- parse_locales(locale)[[1]]
invisible(Sys.setlocale(category = "LC_ALL", locale = locale))
invisible(Sys.setLanguage(localeObj$language))
if(is.null(localeObj$territory)){
  locale_gt <- localeObj$language
  locale_stringi <- localeObj$language
} else {
  locale_gt <- paste0(localeObj$language, "-", localeObj$territory)
  locale_stringi <- paste0(localeObj$language, "_", localeObj$territory)
}
options(`gt.locale` = locale_gt)

sR <- get_string_resources(localeObj)

# ── Confidence interval display ─────────────────────────────────────
# Composable CI components: "pooled", "quartiles".
# Pass individually or combined (comma-separated).
# Convenience values: "all" (= pooled,quartiles), "none" (= empty).
# Default: "all" (Reference Report shows everything).
ci_config <- params$includeConfidenceIntervals
if (is.null(ci_config)) ci_config <- "all"

# Expand convenience values and split comma-separated components
ci_components <- if (identical(ci_config, "all")) {
  c("pooled", "quartiles")
} else if (identical(ci_config, "none")) {
  character(0)
} else {
  trimws(unlist(strsplit(as.character(ci_config), ",")))
}

# Granular flags — Reference Report has no separate "own" rate column.
# The only rate column IS the pooled rate, controlled by include_pooled_ci.
include_pooled_ci   <- "pooled" %in% ci_components
include_quartile_ci <- "quartiles" %in% ci_components

# CI display patterns for cols_merge().  Tables set wrap_ci (TRUE/FALSE)
# to select between wrapped (CI on its own line) and inline display.
# The default is TRUE when multiple CI columns compete for space.
ci_pattern_inline <- "{1} ({2}\u2013{3})"
ci_pattern_wrap   <- if (knitr::is_latex_output()) {
  "{1} \\newline({2}\u2013{3})"
} else {
  "{1}<br>({2}\u2013{3})"
}
# Wrapping is needed when many CI columns compete for space.
# In practice this means quartile CIs are shown (4 CI columns at ~16% each).
wrap_ci_default    <- include_quartile_ci && include_pooled_ci
# `ci_pattern_default` is the stable default. Per-table overrides assign to
# `ci_pattern`, which is shared across chunks; tables that want the default
# back must opt in explicitly via `ci_pattern <- ci_pattern_default`.
ci_pattern_default <- if (wrap_ci_default) ci_pattern_wrap else ci_pattern_inline
ci_pattern         <- ci_pattern_default

# ── Adaptive column widths ─────────────────────────────────────────
# Reference Report layout: stub | N | Pooled(CI) | Q1(CI) | Q2(CI) | Q3(CI)
# 4 data columns (Pooled + 3 quartiles) plus N.
compute_col_widths <- function(tbl_data, stub_min = 25,
                               base_font_size = 11L,
                               bare_w = 15, n_w = 8) {
  pooled_has_ci <- include_pooled_ci &&
    "ci_lower" %in% names(tbl_data) && any(!is.na(tbl_data$ci_lower))
  q_has_ci <- include_quartile_ci &&
    "q1_ci_lower" %in% names(tbl_data) && any(!is.na(tbl_data$q1_ci_lower))

  n_data_cols <- 4L  # pooled + q1 + q2 + q3
  n_ci      <- sum(pooled_has_ci) + 3 * sum(q_has_ci)
  remaining <- 100 - n_w - (n_data_cols - n_ci) * bare_w
  per_ci    <- if (n_ci > 0) min(floor((remaining - stub_min) / n_ci), 25) else 0
  stub_w    <- remaining - n_ci * per_ci

  font_size <- if (ci_config == "none") {
    base_font_size + 1L
  } else if (q_has_ci) {
    base_font_size - 1L
  } else {
    base_font_size
  }

  list(
    stub      = stub_w,
    n         = n_w,
    pooled    = if (pooled_has_ci) per_ci else bare_w,
    q         = if (q_has_ci)      per_ci else bare_w,
    font_size = font_size
  )
}

# Sparse data threshold: rows with fewer than this many events are flagged.
# Default: 16 (relative standard error ≥ 25%, aligned with WA DOH criterion).
# Relative standard error = 1/√n: n=10 → 31.6%, n=16 → 25.0%, n=20 → 22.4%.
sparse_threshold <- as.integer(params$sparseDataThreshold %||% 16L)
```

```{r Importing data}
#| include: false

if(!exists("params")) params <- list()

if (!is.null(params$referenceDataFile)) {
  if (!file.exists(params$referenceDataFile))
    stop(sprintf("The file '%s' does not exist.", params$referenceDataFile))
  reference_data <- jsonlite::unserializeJSON(
    readChar(
        params$referenceDataFile,
        file.info(params$referenceDataFile)$size))
} else {
  reference_data <- with_error_trace(
    import_dhis2(
      connection_options = get_connection_options(
        scheme = params$dhis2Scheme, hostname = params$dhis2Hostname,
        port = params$dhis2Port, path = params$dhis2Path),
      dataset_options = get_dataset_options(
        reportingPeriodFrom = params$reportingPeriodFrom,
        reportingPeriodTo = params$reportingPeriodTo,
        birthWeightFrom = params$birthWeightFrom,
        birthWeightTo = params$birthWeightTo,
        gestationWeeksFrom = params$gestationWeeksFrom,
        gestationWeeksTo = params$gestationWeeksTo,
        reportingCountries = params$reportingCountries,
        testUnitFilter = params$testUnitFilter,
        defaultPatientFilter = params$defaultPatientFilter,
        validationExceptionFile = params$validationExceptionFile)) |>
      neoipcr::calculate_reference_data())
}

```

```{r Department minimum check}
#| include: false
# Warn when fewer than 5 departments contribute — reference data from very
# small groups may expose which departments had specific rare events.
if (reference_data$n_departments < 5L) {
  warning(sprintf(
    paste0("Reference data contains only %d department(s). ",
           "Publishing reference reports based on fewer than 5 departments ",
           "is not recommended due to organizational data protection concerns."),
    reference_data$n_departments))
}
```

```{r Preparing localised data}
#| include: false

ref_counts <- list(
  n_departments = reference_data$n_departments,
  n_patients = reference_data$n_patients$total,
  n_enrollments = reference_data$n_enrollments$total,
  n_patient_days = reference_data$n_patient_days$total,
  n_severe_infections = reference_data$incidence_density_rate_table |>
    dplyr::filter(inf == "si") |> dplyr::pull("n"),
  n_infectious_agents = reference_data$infectious_agent_detection_rate_per_agent_table |>
    dplyr::filter(group == "Total") |> dplyr::pull("n"),
  n_infections_with_agent = reference_data$infectious_agent_detection_rate_per_inf_type_table |>
    dplyr::filter(inf == "all") |> dplyr::pull("n"),
  n_infections_overall = reference_data$n_infections |>
    dplyr::filter(inf_type == "overall") |> dplyr::pull("total"),
  n_surgical_departments = reference_data$n_surgical_departments,
  n_surgical_patients = reference_data$n_surgical_patients |> dplyr::pull("total"),
  # Use the table's Overall row to match the displayed N (excludes "not_surgery")
  n_surgical_procedures = if (!is.null(reference_data$surgery_rate_table))
    reference_data$surgery_rate_table |>
      dplyr::filter(.data$pro_cat == "overall") |> dplyr::pull("n")
    else 0L,
  n_surgical_site_infections = reference_data$n_infections |>
    dplyr::filter(inf_type == "ssi") |> dplyr::pull("total")
)

dR <- c(
  list(creationTimestamp = format(
    lubridate::now("UTC"), format = "%x %X", tz = "UTC", usetz = TRUE)),
  format_dataset_resources(reference_data$metadata, ref_counts, sR)
)

# ── Conditional cross-references ──────────────────────────────────
dR$refDetectionTable <- if (params$includeInfectiousAgentDetectionRateTable)
  " (@tbl-infectious-agent-detection-rate)" else ""
dR$refAgentPerInfTable <- if (params$includeAgentPerInfectionRateTable)
  " (@tbl-agent-per-infection-rate)" else ""
dR$refIDRTable <- if (params$includeIncidenceDensityTable)
  " (@tbl-incidence-density-rates)" else ""
dR$refDevAssTable <- if (params$includeDeviceAssociatedIncidenceDensityTable)
  " (@tbl-device-associated-incidence-density-rates)" else ""

# ── Section visibility flags ─────────────────────────────────────
show_section_surgical    <- params$includeSurgicalProcedureRateTable
show_section_infectious  <- params$includeAgentPerInfectionRateTable ||
  params$includeResistantPathogenInfectionRateTable ||
  params$includeOrganismResistanceRateTable
show_section_diagnostics <- params$includeInfectiousAgentDetectionRateTable ||
  params$includeAntibioticResistanceTestRateTable
show_section_nosocomial  <- params$includeIncidenceDensityTable ||
  params$includeDeviceAssociatedIncidenceDensityTable ||
  params$includeSecondaryBsiRateTable ||
  show_section_infectious || show_section_diagnostics
show_section_risk        <- params$includeRiskDensityRateTable ||
  params$includeAntibioticUtilisationTable ||
  show_section_surgical
show_section_patient_population <- params$includeIntroductionTexts ||
  params$includeMethodsTexts ||
  params$includeBirthWeightFigure ||
  params$includeGestationalAgeFigure

# ── Conditional block helpers ────────────────────────────────────
.show <- function(flag) cat('::: {.content-',
  if (flag) 'visible' else 'hidden',
  ' when-meta="alwaysTrue"}\n', sep = '')
.end <- function() cat(':::\n')

# Detect if reference dataset has mixed World Bank classes
mixed_wb_classes <- detect_mixed_wb_classes(
  ref_countries = reference_data$metadata$countries,
  include_wb_class = reference_data$metadata$dataset_options$include_world_bank_class
)

```

```{r Set up NeoIPC layout}
#| include: false

NeoIPC_pallette <- c("#FF9015","#0083C1","#0D4F6C","#E6FAFC","#E0D3DE","#1F271B")
pal <- c(NeoIPC_pallette[5],NeoIPC_pallette[2],NeoIPC_pallette[6])

```
